articles.title: Q11 — Chronic illness burden
content: Does the elder have serious chronic illness or multiple health conditions?

ul: **Severe or untreated (3)** — severe or progressive illness affecting wellbeing

head: Response
Operational definition
rows: Severe or progressive illness affecting wellbeing.

head: Score
Triggered response
Priority
Owner
Timeframe
rows: PHC/UPHC linkage confirmed; consolidated medication list with family; quarterly clinical review schedule
Urgent medical referral; transport arranged; family briefed; supervisor informed

articles.title: Q12 — Medicine continuity
content: Is the elder taking medicines regularly?

ul: **Not taking despite need (3)** — required medicines not being taken

content: Asking 'Are you taking your medicines?' almost always gets 'yes.' Look — count the pills left against the days passed. The truth is in the strip.

head: Score
Triggered response
Priority
Owner
Timeframe
rows: Diagnose cause: cost / stockout / dislike / forgetting. Address specifically: cost → PHC supply or Janaushadhi; stockout → escalate PHC officer; forgetting → family supervision or pill organiser
Same + family conversation re importance; counselling if elder refuses for psychological reasons; supervisor involvement

articles.title: Q13a — Sensory function (vision and hearing)
content: Does the elder have vision or hearing problems affecting daily life? Tick which: ☐ Vision  ☐ Hearing  ☐ Both. Score reflects the more severe of the two.

ul: **No issues (0)** — no issues, or fully corrected with glasses/hearing aid
**Moderate (2)** — significantly affects daily activity; cataract surgery or hearing aid candidate
**Severe (3)** — functionally blind, profoundly deaf, or compounding other disability — **CRITICAL FLAG**

content: Test simply — can the elder read a newspaper headline at arm's length (vision)? Does the elder ask for repetition or speak loudly without realising (hearing)? Cataract and hearing loss are major preventable disability drivers in Indian slum elders; both operable.

articles.title: Q13a — Operational definitions
content: Q13a added in v4 because cataract and hearing loss are major preventable disability drivers in Indian slum elders. Cataract prevalence reaches 50–60% in over-70 populations, mostly operable. Hearing loss drives social withdrawal, family conflict, and cognitive decline, and is addressable with a basic hearing aid.

head: Response
Operational definition
rows: No issues, or fully corrected with glasses/hearing aid.
Significantly affects daily activity; cataract surgery or hearing aid candidate.
Functionally blind, profoundly deaf, or compounding other disability.

head: Score
Triggered response
Priority
Owner
Timeframe
rows: Refer to next eye/ENT camp (or set one up via partner); disability certificate workflow opened if eligible (referral to Entitlements Team)
**Critical flag review.** Priority surgical referral (cataract) or hearing aid procurement; disability certificate fast-track

articles.title: Q13b — Immediate medical attention
content: Does the elder currently require immediate medical attention?

ul: **Urgent attention needed (3)** — immediate medical response required — **CRITICAL FLAG**

head: Score
Triggered response
Priority
Owner
Timeframe
rows: Specialist referral with accompaniment if elder alone; transport arranged; follow-up within 14 days of referral
**Critical flag — Acute Deterioration protocol.** Same-day PHC contact; 108 if life-threatening
